title: The Macro Minute: Will Mag-7 earnings and US Treasury’s debt issuance plans interrupt Paradigm C?

Darius Dell to present our macro minute
for Tuesday July 29th 2025. As always
we'll start with the executive summary
from today's lead off morning note. If
you would like to review the analysis
supporting these conclusions and to see
if you need to make any changes in your
portfolio to remain on the right side of
market risk or to get on the right side
of market risk to the extent you haven't
been following Kiss or Dr. Mo need to be
a client of Freddy Tacro. So today's key
macro questions are will MAG7 earnings
interrupt our paradigm C party and will
the US Treasury's debt issuance plans
interrupt our paradigm C party? Our
answers somewhat unlikely as it relates
to Max 7 earnings and highly unlikely as
it relates to the Treasury's debt
issuance plans. Meta, Microsoft, Amazon,
and Apple worth a combined 11 trillion
dollars and comprising roughly 20% of
the S&P 500 report earnings this week in
a pivotal test to whether increased
investor optimism regarding corporate
sales and earnings growth uh which is a
key support of our paradigm C rally is
justified at 28 times Ford earnings. The
Magnificent 7 have rebounded from
April's tariffled route but remain well
below December's 34 times peak
valuation. Moreover, the S&P 100 trades
at 22 times earnings. As most recently
discussed in our July 26 around the
horn, legacy valuation threshold should
continue to be disregarded by long-term
investors. By the end of this decade,
every investor will learn what team 42
already knows. Valuation is a
non-stationary time series during a
technological revolution. Elsewhere,
there were no big surprises in
yesterday's opening volley of the Q3 US
Treasury quarterly refunding
announcement. Since Scott Besson became
Treasury Secretary in January, he has
successfully executed a complete about
face regarding his issuance playbook in
the $ 29 trillion US Treasury market.
Initially expected to ramp up long-term
issuance after criticizing Janet Yellen
for election year yield suppression,
Besson has since fully embraced Yellen's
activist Treasury issuance, citing
unattractively high yields. We continue
to believe the administration's desire
to engineer economic boom to hopefully
outrun the US's fiscal problems, i.e.
Paradigm C is the key reason why Besson
has adopted our fellow Yale Janet
Yellen's policy after lambasting it in
the media for 12 to 18 months. Besset
may use part two of the Q3 QR to clarify
how far he's willing to let bills grow
as a share of US debt with the Treasury
Borrowing Advisory Committee Committee
uh recommending a 15 to 20% range over
time. In our deeply researched view,
this range must and likely will rise
over the next 5 to 10 years as the
geopolitically driven supply demand
imbalance in the Treasury market grows
amid the deepening forth turning poly
crisis we have helped team 42 profitably
position for for years. Transitioning to
our 42 micro dashboard as always wrap up
the question from our community. This
one is regarding our our our KISS

back uh in all caps. So appreciate you.
So uh just a general rule of thumb in
terms of riskmanaging financial markets,
you never want to allocate a bunch of
capital too quickly. Uh for a variety of
reasons uh most notably uh our believe
uh you know the illusion of validity
convinces us that we are a lot smarter
than the market and over time as you
gain experience and gray hairs as an
investor uh you realize that you're not
smarter than the market. So uh the
illusion of validity is that that that
bias that triggers you into okay I got
to get fully invested. I would add the
action bias is contributo uh to that as
well as is the optimism bias. There's a
lot of behavior horistics involved in
why you would um you know quickly uh uh
rush panic buy any asset. Uh do not
panic buy assets. Uh what you should do
instead uh is to dollar cost average
anytime you're accumulating an asset and
and and moving this tremendous amount of
sums in your portfolio. On the buy side
on the sales side is very different. the
sales side. We know from our
institutional background, me circum

two decades now, meeting with many of
the top portfolio managers and CIOS
across global Wall Street and very a

learned is that and also just from
experience of trading and risk
management markets buy in my own
portfolio markets take the window down
and the escalator up and so when you get
a sell signal like the kind of uh you
know timely actionable sales signals
that are generated by the risk
management overlays that we feature in
KISS uh and Dr. when you get a sale
signal, you should react to that quickly
and expeditiously uh because ultimately
that puts you in line ahead of the other
investors that will eventually be forced
to sell to CYA their own uh portfolios.
Uh uh but on the buy side things uh move
a little bit move much slower uh and so
you have a little bit of time to wait.

congruent with 42 macro research summary
or quantitative risk management summary?
Uh and then uh because for those who may
not be unfamiliar, we keep our research
fundamental research process and our
quantitative risk management signaling
processes completely separate like just
like church and state. What I think
about growth, inflation, monetary
policy, fiscal policy, liquidity and
positioning lives here in this house.
What should you be doing in your
portfolio about all that lives here in
this house? And the when the two meet,
you know, if we're forecasting
appropriately and and measuring and
mapping where we are in each of those
cycles well at a high level, then the
kiss and dr signals will just evolve in
the way that our forecasts anticipate.
However, the reason we and the top funds
across global Wall Street keep
fundamental research completely separate
from their quantitative risk management
signaling is because when you are wrong
fundamentally, it takes way more time to
recognize the mistake and correct the
mistake in your portfolio than it does
to just just passionately execute a
riskmanagement signal. that either helps
your portfolio reposition for uh uh uh
you know minimizing downside capture in
in a pending bare market or the the same
signals will help you reposition your
portfolio to maximize upside capture in
a pending bull market. It's the same
process. And so what we're trying to do
is speed up the amount of time it takes
for us to notice big changes in the
markets. What we call rorow row phase
transitions going from risk on to risk
off. Um there's further delineations
there but that's really what matters to
retail investors. you know, we want to
notice those changes faster than other
investors so we can buy what they
eventually are going to be forced to buy
before them or sell what they're
eventually going to be forced to sell uh
before them. We did that twice already
this year. We sold what they were
eventually going to be forced to sell
starting in late February and concluded
all that those sales in early March,
first week of March. And then we bought
start buying again uh what they were
going to be forced to buy starting in uh
midFebruary sorry mid April in Bitcoin
in late April uh with the S&P 500 um
your maximum gold throughout. And so you
know this is this is why we do what we
